issues: 1x item icons dissolve into colored blobs — individual silhouettes unreadable at native scale
mid-row grey cloud/blob icons have ambiguous silhouettes even at 4x
empty slot rectangles are generic with zero personality
dark-bg and light-bg variants appear nearly identical — panel does not adapt contrast to context
reasoning: The inventory panel carries genuine toy-chest charm: warm earthy pixel art palette, ornate golden border with circular corner ornaments, and a storybook-adventure aesthetic that aligns well with Echoes of Wisdom reference. Rounded shapes (tree, carrot, character, mushroom, red gem/heart) read as friendly and soft. Panel-level silhouette at 1x is clear — the gold-bordered parchment rectangle punches through the dark grey background. However, the strict 'instantly readable at 1x' criterion fails for individual items: icons collapse to indistinct colored dots at native scale, making the inventory contents unidentifiable without zooming in. At 4x the stronger icons (tree, lightning bolt, gem) pass the sticker test; weaker ones (oval grey blobs, row-2 cloud shapes) do not have distinct enough outlines to name at a glance. Empty slot placeholders are utilitarian grey rectangles — no rounded warmth, no personality. Net: charm is real and the panel-level read is solid, but item-level silhouette clarity at 1x keeps this in marginal territory.
